title: "Iran demands $1 per barrel of oil passing through Strait of Hormuz, paid in crypto - The Hill"

---

Iran has announced a new policy requiring $1 per barrel for oil passing through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, with payments to be made in cryptocurrency. This move comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and aims to bolster Iran's economy, which has been significantly impacted by sanctions and fluctuating oil prices.

The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for global oil supply, with approximately 20% of the world's oil passing through it. Iran's demand could disrupt oil shipping routes and raise concerns over supply security, potentially leading to increased oil prices. Market participants should closely monitor how this policy could influence oil futures and the broader energy sector, particularly if it prompts retaliatory measures from other nations or affects shipping insurance costs.

One key takeaway for investors is the potential for heightened volatility in oil markets, as geopolitical developments in the region could lead to supply chain disruptions and impact global oil pricing dynamics.

Source: [news.google.com](https://news.google.?oc=5)
